domain_experience: revenue leakage identification in provider groups
assets: deep knowledge of denial and prior auth failure modes
ability to translate operational pain into product requirements
ability to build lightweight AI-enabled tools
credibility_signals: staff engineer at a healthcare claims startup
firsthand knowledge of denial and prior auth economics
unfair_advantages: better problem selection than a generic AI founder
can identify where provider groups actually lose money
can design credible workflow-specific AI outputs

opportunities.title: Denial and Prior Auth Playbook Pack for Independent Provider Groups
opportunities.thesis: Sell self-serve digital packs with denial taxonomy, appeal prompt templates, prior auth SOPs, and AI-ready workflow prompts for specialty practices and small RCM teams.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user knows which denial and auth patterns actually cost money, so the product can focus on operationally valuable failure modes instead of generic advice.
validation_plan: Create one specialty-specific pack using only public knowledge and synthetic examples
Publish a landing page with async checkout
Test willingness to pay in niche operator communities
kill_criteria: No paid interest from target buyers after narrow async distribution
Buyers view the pack as generic or non-actionable

opportunities.title: Payer Policy Change Digest for High-Denial Specialties
opportunities.thesis: Publish a paid async digest for a narrow specialty, translating public payer bulletins into operational implications.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can separate policy changes that matter operationally from document noise, which generic summarizers cannot do well.
validation_plan: Publish one free sample issue for a narrow specialty
Offer paid subscription or annual access
Measure subscriber conversion and retention intent
kill_criteria: Low buyer response to workflow-oriented policy updates
High content effort with weak recurring demand

opportunities.title: Prior Auth Requirements Copilot
opportunities.thesis: Build a self-serve tool that turns public payer policy and auth rules into searchable answers, checklist outputs, and submission prep guidance for staff.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can design a workflow-native product instead of a generic document chatbot, making the output more credible to operations teams.
validation_plan: Prototype answers for one specialty and 3 to 5 payers
Check whether users trust outputs enough to pay
kill_criteria: Users do not trust public-policy-only outputs
Keeping guidance current becomes too burdensome

opportunities.title: Denial Opportunity Benchmark Database
opportunities.thesis: Create a subscription benchmark product showing denial and auth leakage patterns by specialty, payer, and workflow stage using only public or user-contributed non-PHI inputs.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user knows how to structure denial and auth categories in a way that can become a useful benchmark rather than an arbitrary dashboard.
validation_plan: Start with synthetic or contributed de-identified sample sets
Test whether buyers care about comparative benchmarks or only internal insights
Assess if enough data can be sourced legally
kill_criteria: Cannot gather enough legal non-PHI data to create signal
Benchmark concept is interesting but not purchase-worthy

opportunities.title: Specialty-Specific Denial Leakage Analyzer: Toolkit to Async SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Start with a paid self-serve spreadsheet/template plus AI prompt workflow for one specialty, then evolve into a lightweight SaaS that ingests de-identified denial exports and recommends next actions.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can define the right denial categories, missed-documentation patterns, and intervention logic from lived domain knowledge, which is the core of product usefulness.
validation_plan: Choose one specialty and buyer type
Build a no-PHI CSV template and output report
Launch async landing page and test for paid beta demand
Use only de-identified buyer-supplied data or synthetic examples
tool_specs: CSV upload template with non-PHI fields only
prioritized leakage report with recommended interventions
kill_criteria: No paid beta or preorder interest after focused async outreach
Useful output cannot be produced from de-identified data
Buyers only want bespoke consulting or meetings

opportunities.title: Public Payer Policy Tracker to Workflow Copilot
opportunities.thesis: Start with a paid async policy tracker for one specialty, then add alerts, task generation, and workflow recommendations as a recurring product.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user understands the operational consequence of policy shifts, which is what turns commodity summarization into a valuable workflow tool.
validation_plan: Launch a narrow tracker for one specialty
Sell paid access to early subscribers
Add recurring operational recommendations based on subscriber feedback
kill_criteria: Users value updates but will not pay recurring fees
The product becomes a commodity newsletter

next_actions: Review employment agreement, invention assignment, moonlighting, and non-solicit terms
Choose one specialty and one buyer persona
Build a no-PHI CSV template and synthetic sample dataset
Create a landing page with async checkout and a 2-minute demo
Test paid demand with non-employer-conflicted prospects through async channels only
